channel.title: Chart Breakers & Star Stories
channel.itunes:author: Family TVR
channel.itunes:summary: Did your favorite artist just break a record or a heart? The charts are screaming, and we have the inside story you won't hear anywhere else.
channel.description: The latest music trends, chart updates, and star stories from Billboard and beyond. Stay updated with the Pop Culture.

item.title: Ella Langley Brings Out Theo Von for Surprise ‘You Look Like You Love Me’ Duet at Stagecoach 2026
item.description:
[Energetic music fades out, and the host's voice takes over]
Host: "Welcome to Trending on Billboard. I'm your host, and today we're talking about a surprise Stagecoach debut that's got everyone buzzing. Country star Ella Langley took the stage in Indio, California, on April 24th, and she had a trick up her sleeve."
[Soft music transitions in the background]
Host: "Instead of bringing out Morgan Wallen, as some fans might have expected, Langley invited comedian and podcaster Theo Von to join her on stage. Together, they performed a duet of 'You Look Like You Love Me', her hit collaboration with Riley Green."
[Clip of "You Look Like You Love Me" plays briefly in the background]
Host: "The crowd went wild as Von, dressed in a denim shirt, blue jeans, and a cowboy hat, jumped into the second verse. And the chemistry between him and Langley was undeniable. They even got the festivalgoers singing along to the catchy chorus."
[Clip of the crowd singing along plays briefly]
Host: "This surprise performance comes on the heels of Langley's new album, Dandelion, which debuted at No. 1 on the Billboard 200. And she's not slowing down – her song 'Choosin' Texas' is currently dominating the Billboard Hot 100, marking its seventh non-consecutive week at the top."
[Soft music continues to play]
Host: "Langley's also been making waves with her new duet, 'I Can't Love You Anymore', with Morgan Wallen, which she premiered during his stadium show in Alabama on April 18th. It's clear that Ella Langley is on a roll, and we can't wait to see what's next for this country star."
[Music transitions to a more upbeat tone]
Host: "That's all for today's episode of Trending on Billboard. Thanks for tuning in, and we'll catch you on the next one."
[Music and host continue for another 2-3 seconds before fading out]
